resources.string: Formula: EV₁₀₀ = log₂(E / 2.5) | C = 250 (ANSI/ISO, flat sensor)
Select the film to apply its reciprocity failure correction.
Choose the aperture: the app calculates the exposure time
Choose the shutter speed: the app calculates the aperture
E is the illuminance in lux measured by the light meter. The constant 2.5 equals C / 100, where C = 250 is the ANSI PH3.49-1971 / ISO 2720:1974 calibration constant for flat (cosine) sensors.
Doubling the ISO increases the exposure by +1 EV (one full stop).
N is the f-number (aperture) and t is the exposure time in seconds.

Given EV and N:
t = N² / 2^EV

Given EV and t:
N = √(t × 2^EV)
These two forms drive the A (aperture priority) and B (shutter priority) modes of the app.
All combinations (N, t) satisfying the same EV produce the same exposure. The equivalent combinations table demonstrates this principle.

Reciprocity law: H = E × t (valid ~1/1000 s – 1 s on film)

Schwarzschild effect: response ∝ E × t^p, p < 1

Ilford/HARMAN model:
Tc = Tm^P (no correction for Tm ≤ 1 s)

Datasheet tables (Kodak, Foma):
log-log linear interpolation of (Tm → Tc) points

Stop-based data (Fuji, E100):
Tc = Tm × 2^s(Tc) (fixed point)

Shutter-priority (fixed actual time t):
Tm_eq = t / 2^s(t) or t^(1/P) → N = √(Tm_eq × 2^EV)

On film, effective sensitivity drops during long exposures (low-intensity reciprocity failure): the metered time Tm must be extended to the corrected time Tc. The Analog tab applies the official manufacturer data per film stock; beyond the published data the value is extrapolated and flagged. High-intensity failure (< 1/10,000 s) is outside the app range. Digital sensors are linear: no correction.

C = 250 applies to flat (cosine) sensors. Hemispherical sensors use C = 330 (Minolta) or C = 340 (Sekonic). Standard f-numbers follow the ISO photographic scale (including 1/3 and 1/2 stops); shutter speeds range from 30s to 1/8000s. Comparisons are logarithmic to match exposure stops.

• Wikipedia — "Exposure value": formal EV definition and lux/EV reference table

• ANSI PH3.49-1971 — calibration constant C = 250

• ISO 2720:1974 — international light meter standard

• ANSI PH2.7-1986 — EV reference table for real-world scenes

• HARMAN Technology — "Film Reciprocity Failure Compensation" (Dec 2023): Tc = Tm^P factors for Ilford/Kentmere films

• Kodak F-4016 (T-Max 100), F-4017 (Tri-X), F-4043 (T-Max 400) — long exposure adjustment tables

• Fujifilm AF3-0258E (Acros II), RVP50 Data Guide (Velvia 50), Provia 100F datasheet

• Foma — Fomapan 100/400 datasheets (Schwarzschild effect tables)
